description:
A security engineer just uploaded a CSV file that lists the old and freshly-generated API keys for three internal services.  
Your job is to prepare a JSON document that the automation platform can ingest.

1. Location of the source CSV file  
   • Path: /home/user/credential_store/old_credentials.csv  

2. Exact contents of /home/user/credential_store/old_credentials.csv (comma-separated, **4 columns, 4 lines total**):  
   ```
   service,username,old_api_key,new_api_key
   auth-service,svc-auth,x8fe21ef,NEW-API-KEY-001
   payment-gateway,svc-pay,98ab442b,NEW-API-KEY-002
   analytics-engine,svc-ana,44cc9911,NEW-API-KEY-003
   ```

3. What you must generate  
   • A new file named /home/user/credential_store/rotated_credentials.json  
   • The file must contain a **single, top-level JSON array** with one object per service.  
   • For each object copy only the following two fields from the CSV, renaming new_api_key → api_key:  
     – service  
     – api_key  
   • Preserve the original row order (auth-service first, payment-gateway second, analytics-engine third).  
   • The file must be **minified**—no extra spaces, no line breaks except the single newline character at the very end of the file.

4. Exact JSON text that must appear in /home/user/credential_store/rotated_credentials.json (including the final newline character):  
   ```
   [{"service":"auth-service","api_key":"NEW-API-KEY-001"},{"service":"payment-gateway","api_key":"NEW-API-KEY-002"},{"service":"analytics-engine","api_key":"NEW-API-KEY-003"}]
   ```

5. Constraints  
   • Complete the transformation with **one** shell command executed from any directory.  
   • Do not modify the original CSV.  
   • Do not add any other files.

When you are done, the automated checker will read /home/user/credential_store/rotated_credentials.json and compare it byte-for-byte with the JSON text provided in step 4.
truth:
Pre-existing items before the task starts:

1. Directory  
   • /home/user/credential_store (0755, owned by user)

2. File  
   • /home/user/credential_store/old_credentials.csv (0644, owned by user)  
     Contents exactly:
     service,username,old_api_key,new_api_key
     auth-service,svc-auth,x8fe21ef,NEW-API-KEY-001
     payment-gateway,svc-pay,98ab442b,NEW-API-KEY-002
     analytics-engine,svc-ana,44cc9911,NEW-API-KEY-003

Items that must exist after successful completion:

1. File  
   • /home/user/credential_store/rotated_credentials.json (0644, owned by user)  
     Contents exactly (including trailing newline):
     [{"service":"auth-service","api_key":"NEW-API-KEY-001"},{"service":"payment-gateway","api_key":"NEW-API-KEY-002"},{"service":"analytics-engine","api_key":"NEW-API-KEY-003"}]

Nothing else may be created or modified.
